race.name: Bieg Podhalański
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers through the Tatra Mountain foothills — where Poland's highland culture, Góral traditions, and reliable Carpathian snow create a distinct Polish ski marathon.

climate.description: February in the Tatra foothills at 900m brings reliable Carpathian winter conditions. Temperatures typically -10°C to 0°C. The Tatras act as a snow barrier, receiving heavy precipitation from southern weather systems. The Podhale plateau retains snow well.
challenges: Föhn wind (halny) can bring sudden warming from the south
Variable conditions in the Carpathian climate
Mountain weather can change rapidly in the Tatra region
Altitude of 900m adds modest physiological stress

course.primary: Course through the Podhale region at the foot of the Tatra Mountains. Rolling terrain across the highland plateau with views of the Tatra peaks. The Podhale is Poland's distinctive highland region — open meadows, spruce forest, and traditional wooden villages. More varied terrain than lowland Polish courses.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through the Podhale highland
features: Tatra Mountain panorama as a dramatic backdrop
Podhale — Poland's distinctive highland region with Góral culture
Traditional wooden architecture along the route
Carpathian mountain climate provides reliable snow
Both classic and skate technique available

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Tatra Mountain scenery and Góral highland culture give this race distinctive character. Proximity to Zakopane provides good infrastructure and accessibility. Shorter 30km distance and modest international draw limit top scores. Solid regional event in Poland's premier winter sports area.

history.summary: The Bieg Podhalański is a ski marathon in the Podhale region of southern Poland, at the foot of the Tatra Mountains. The Podhale is home to the Góral people — Poland's highland culture with distinctive music, architecture, and traditions. Zakopane, nearby, is Poland's undisputed winter capital and has hosted numerous international ski competitions. The race celebrates the region's deep connection to winter sports and mountain culture.
notable_facts: Podhale is home to the Góral people — Poland's highland mountain culture
Near Zakopane — Poland's winter capital since the 19th century
The Tatra Mountains are the highest range in the Carpathians
Zakopane hosted the 1929 and 1939 FIS Nordic World Ski Championships
Poland's Bieg Piastów (Jakuszyce) is the country's largest ski marathon — Bieg Podhalański offers the Tatra alternative
The Podhale's traditional wooden architecture is a UNESCO-recognized heritage
